document:
MERIDIAN CRYPTO ALPHA FUND I — Confidential Offering Memorandum (Fragment)

The Fund: Meridian Crypto Alpha Fund I is a Cayman Islands exempted limited partnership offering interests to qualified purchasers and accredited investors. The Fund will pursue alpha generation in digital asset markets through a combination of long-only directional positioning, basis trading, and selective participation in pre-launch token allocations.

Manager: The Fund is managed by Meridian Digital Holdings, LP, formed in 2026. The principal of Meridian Digital Holdings is Sergei Volkov, who founded the firm in late 2025 after departing from a global macro hedge fund. Mr. Volkov has approximately 4 years of relevant trading experience.

Strategy Notes: The Fund's strategy does not fit cleanly into traditional alternative asset classifications. While elements of the strategy resemble systematic hedge fund approaches, the underlying asset class (digital assets) and the inclusion of pre-launch token allocations represent a hybrid approach not commonly seen in private fund structures.

Fund Economics: Management fee will be charged at 2.5% per annum on net asset value. The Fund will charge a performance fee of 30% of net profits, calculated quarterly with no high water mark provision. Performance fees crystallize quarterly and are paid out within 30 days of each quarter-end.

Minimum Investment: The minimum LP commitment is $250,000.

Lockup and Redemption: Investors are subject to a 12-month hard lockup followed by quarterly redemption windows requiring 90 days advance notice. The General Partner reserves the right to gate redemptions if aggregate redemption requests in any quarter exceed 25% of the Fund's NAV.

Fund Size and Term: The Fund has no stated target size or hard cap. The Fund has no stated term and operates as an open-ended vehicle.

Vintage and Inception: The Fund's first close is anticipated in Q3 2026, with subscriptions accepted on a rolling basis thereafter.

Key Risks: The Fund's strategy involves significant directional risk in digital asset markets, which have experienced drawdowns in excess of 75% in prior cycles. The Fund's pre-launch token allocations may include tokens that have not been registered as securities and whose regulatory status is uncertain. The General Partner is not a registered investment adviser and operates under the de minimis exemption. The Fund's auditor and administrator have not yet been engaged as of the date of this memorandum.
